title: Reply with exactly: explorer OK
summary_text: Findings in `scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh`: 1. Current stale logic still short-circuits on age+PID only, so a claim can be released even when compact/tmux evidence shows it is still active. 2. Active-liveness signals are not unified; PID-only checks miss the two required safety gates for your request (`tmux` session + compact freshness). Minimal safe stale predicate for release (in evaluation order): - `claim_active == true` (or equivalent non-terminal active check) - `status != "terminal"` - `now_epoch - updated_at_epoch >= ttl_seconds` - `run_driver_pid is not a live process` (`kill -0` false or missing/invalid PID) - Resolve compact path from claim: - `claim.compact_artifact_path` → workspace receipt `.compact_artifact_path` → `compact_path_for_run(run_id)` - `compact_tmux_session is not live` - parse `.tmux_session` from compact JSON - `tmux has-session -t "=<session>"` must be false/absent - if it returns live (or is unknown because session check is unavailable), treat as non-stale (do not release) - `compact_updated_epoch` must be missing/older than ttl (not fresher) - if compact has valid `updated_at`, require `now - compact_updated_epoch >= ttl_seconds` - if `updated_at` cannot be parsed, treat as unknown and do not release (safest default) Concrete condition order (single canonical function): 1) `claim not terminal` 2) `age >= ttl` 3) `pid not live` 4) `compact session not live (or unknown => do not release)` 5) `compact fresh check says stale` Only then release. This keeps the “PID gone” path safe: disappearance of PID alone is insufficient without compact-session and compact-age confirmation.
